mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals an ongoing monetary tightening cycle, which raises the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic recession, which typically leads to declining corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX does not show significantly negative 60d returns following a rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive or flat on average when both RSAFS and HOUST show concurrent month-over-month declines.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, forcing the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance. This environment is detrimental to equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive on average during periods where CPIAUCSL is above 5% year-over-year, contradicting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This reduction in output directly translates to lower corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative following a month-over-month decline in INDPRO greater than 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained negative GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates an economic recession, which directly translates to lower corporate earnings, reduced consumer spending, and increased investor pessimism, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 60% of instances where GDPC1 shows sustained negative growth, despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a severe deterioration in the labor market. This reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive or flat on average when PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises concurrently, contradicting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising rapidly while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening in response to persistent inflation. This severely dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive on average during periods of aggressive FEDFUNDS hikes when CPIAUCSL is above 4% year-over-year.
